channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Abortion pills "just in case"? Planned Parenthood will offer them in two states
item.description: In Washington state and Hawaii, residents can now get mifepristone and misoprostol from Planned Parenthood to keep in their cabinets in case they need to end a pregnancy at a later time.
item.content:encoded:
In Washington state and Hawaii, residents can now get mifepristone and misoprostol from Planned Parenthood to keep in their cabinets in case they need to end a pregnancy at a later time.

(Image credit: Ted S. Warren)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Smokers in China are being chided by anti-smoking women
item.description: Many many many men smoke in China. A small but outspoken group of women are becoming anti-smoking advocates, confronting those who light up in public.
item.content:encoded:
Many many many men smoke in China. A small but outspoken group of women are becoming anti-smoking advocates, confronting those who light up in public.

(Image credit: Johannes Eisele/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: COVID is shaping Americans' reaction to Ebola and hantavirus
item.description: Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.
item.content:encoded:
Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: U.S. aid cuts may have delayed detecting this Ebola outbreak
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in Africa was announced last week, then quickly declared an emergency. It's likely that cuts in U.S. aid contributed to a delay in identifying the outbreak.
item.content:encoded: The Ebola outbreak in Africa was announced last week, then quickly declared an emergency. It's likely that cuts in U.S. aid contributed to a delay in identifying the outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Ebola fears surge on the ground in Congo over rapid spread of a rare type
item.description: Healthcare workers in eastern Congo said Wednesday they are underprotected and undertrained in a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak of a rare type of the virus in one of the world's most vulnerable places.
item.content:encoded:
Healthcare workers in eastern Congo said Wednesday they are underprotected and undertrained in a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak of a rare type of the virus in one of the world's most vulnerable places.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: RFK Jr. fires two leaders of preventive health panel
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is shaking up another important scientific panel, the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force.
item.content:encoded: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is shaking up another important scientific panel, the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: How to help children cope after shootings like the San Diego mosque killings
item.description: Children closest to an incident of gun violence have the most risk of lasting psychological effects. Here's what all parents should know about how to buffer trauma.
item.content:encoded:
Children closest to an incident of gun violence have the most risk of lasting psychological effects. Here's what all parents should know about how to buffer trauma.

(Image credit: Gregory Bull)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: A rare Ebola strain is spreading with no vaccine. Here's what you need to know
item.description: This outbreak is being called "the perfect storm." How did it start, what are the characteristics of the strain that's causing it and how much of a threat is it to global health?
item.content:encoded: This outbreak is being called "the perfect storm." How did it start, what are the characteristics of the strain that's causing it and how much of a threat is it to global health?
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Overworked and understaffed: Special ed teachers turn to AI for help
item.description: A fast-growing number of special educators nationwide are using AI to create customized education plans. Despite the risks, some research shows it could improve the quality of teachers' work.
item.content:encoded:
A fast-growing number of special educators nationwide are using AI to create customized education plans. Despite the risks, some research shows it could improve the quality of teachers' work.

(Image credit: Talia Herman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: WHO chief concerned over 'scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization top official has expressed concern over the rapid spread of a rare type of Ebola in Congo. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and over 500 cases.
item.content:encoded:
The World Health Organization top official has expressed concern over the rapid spread of a rare type of Ebola in Congo. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and over 500 cases.

(Image credit: Dirole Lotsima Dieudonne)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
